cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dene: noun. A valley, especially the deep valley of a stream or rivulet.
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dey: noun. A servant who has charge of the dairy; a dairymaid.
dye: noun. A colorant, especially one that has an affinity to the substrate to which it is applied.
dyne: noun. A unit of force in the CGS system; the force required to accelerate a mass of one gram by one centimetre per second per second.
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyne: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
nene: noun. The Hawaiian goose, Branta sandvicensis, which was designated the state bird of Hawaii in 1957.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tendency: noun. A likelihood of behaving in a particular way or going in a particular direction; a tending toward.
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
tyee: noun. A tribal chief of the Chinookan peoples in the Pacific Northwest of the United States.
tyned: verb. simple past and past participle of tyne
yen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Yendang.
yenned: verb. simple past and past participle of yen
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
